title: Velxio Arduino Emulator
description: A guided tour of Velxio — a fully local, open-source Arduino emulator with React frontend, FastAPI backend, and real AVR8/RP2040 emulation powered by Wokwi libraries.

steps.title: Welcome to Velxio
steps.description: Velxio is a full-stack Arduino emulator. The frontend (React + Vite + TypeScript) provides a Monaco code editor and visual simulation canvas. The backend (FastAPI + Python) compiles Arduino sketches via arduino-cli. Real AVR8 and RP2040 emulation runs entirely in the browser using Wokwi open-source libraries.

steps.title: Frontend Application Core
steps.description: The frontend is a React + TypeScript app built with Vite. App.tsx sets up routing, main.tsx is the entry point, and the pages/ directory contains route-level components like the editor, landing page, auth pages, and user profiles.

steps.title: Zustand State Management
steps.description: Application state is managed with Zustand stores. useEditorStore handles the multi-file workspace (files, tabs, active file). useSimulatorStore tracks simulation state, components, wires, and compiled hex. useAuthStore persists auth state in localStorage. useProjectStore tracks the current project being edited.

steps.title: Code Editor & File Management
steps.description: The editor/ directory contains the Monaco-based code editor with multi-file support. CodeEditor.tsx renders Monaco with per-file undo history. FileExplorer.tsx provides the sidebar file tree with rename, delete, and save. FileTabs.tsx manages open file tabs with unsaved-change indicators. EditorToolbar.tsx has the compile, run, and stop buttons.

steps.title: AVR & RP2040 Simulation Engine
steps.description: The simulation/ directory is the heart of Velxio. AVRSimulator.ts wraps the avr8js CPU emulator — it loads hex files, runs the 16MHz execution loop at ~60 FPS, and fires port listeners when GPIO registers change. PinManager.ts maps Arduino pins to visual components (e.g., pin 13 → built-in LED). RP2040Simulator.ts provides similar support for Raspberry Pi Pico boards.

steps.title: Visual Electronic Components
steps.description: Velxio renders 48+ electronic components (LEDs, resistors, buttons, displays, etc.) using Wokwi Web Components. The components-wokwi/ directory contains React wrappers for these custom elements. The simulator/ directory has SimulatorCanvas.tsx which renders the visual canvas, component picker, and board selector. DynamicComponent.tsx handles dynamic rendering of any component type.

steps.title: Backend API (FastAPI + Python)
steps.description: The backend is a FastAPI application that handles Arduino code compilation, authentication, and project persistence. The api/routes/ directory contains endpoints for compilation (compile.py), auth (auth.py), projects (projects.py), and library management (libraries.py). The core/ directory has configuration, JWT security, and dependency injection.

steps.title: Wokwi Open-Source Libraries
steps.description: Velxio uses local clones of official Wokwi repositories instead of npm packages. avr8js/ provides the AVR8 CPU emulator that runs Arduino sketches in the browser. wokwi-elements/ contains the Web Components for visual electronic parts. rp2040js/ provides RP2040 (Raspberry Pi Pico) emulation. Vite aliases in vite.config.ts resolve imports to these local builds.

steps.title: End-to-End Data Flow
steps.description: Here's how it all connects: (1) User writes Arduino code in Monaco Editor → stored in useEditorStore. (2) Compile button sends files to the FastAPI backend → arduino-cli produces a .hex file. (3) The hex file is loaded into AVRSimulator → parsed into CPU instructions. (4) The 60 FPS execution loop runs CPU cycles → port listeners fire on GPIO changes. (5) PinManager maps pin state changes to visual components → React re-renders wokwi-elements on the canvas. The frontend services/ directory bridges the API calls between frontend stores and backend endpoints.
